dev_intro.title: Introduction
dev_intro.description: Build complete applications from concept to deployment with an intelligent, agent-driven platform. Design, develop, test, secure, and launch—all in one place—while maintaining full human oversight at every stage.

f1.title: Design Before Code
f1.desc: Plan your application thoroughly before writing a single line of code. Define the UI, system architecture, and functional specifications upfront to ensure a clear development roadmap and reduce costly iterations later.

f2.title: Voice & Chat Driven
f2.desc: Interact with intelligent AI agents using natural voice or text commands. Build, modify, and refine your app in real time, making development intuitive, fast, and accessible even without deep programming expertise.

f3.title: Post-Deployment Agents
f3.desc: Your AI assistants don't stop working when your app goes live. Continuously monitor, optimize, and enhance your application after deployment, ensuring it evolves alongside user needs and market trends.

f4.title: Human Oversight
f4.desc: Maintain full control throughout the development lifecycle. Review every decision, approve updates, and guide the AI, combining automation with human judgment for reliable and high-quality results.

dev_dashboard.title: Dashboard
dev_dashboard.intro: This dashboard provides a clear, organized view of all your projects in one place. Key features include:
dev_dashboard.f1_desc: Each project is displayed as a card showing the project name and a brief description. This allows for quick recognition and easy access.
dev_dashboard.f2_desc: A search bar at the top lets you find projects quickly by name.
dev_dashboard.f3_desc: Easily start a new project with the "Create New Project" button or log out with a single click.
dev_dashboard.f4_desc: Clicking on a project card takes you directly to that project's workspace for further management and development.
dev_dashboard.f5_desc: The interface is simple and intuitive, focusing on productivity without distractions.
dev_dashboard.f6_desc: Displays the total number of projects at the bottom right, helping users track their workload.
dev_dashboard.outro: This dashboard acts as a central hub for managing, accessing, and launching all your ongoing projects efficiently.

dev_create_project.title: Create a New Project
dev_create_project.how_to_title: How to Create a New Project
dev_create_project.step1: In the Dashboard, click on Create a New Project button
dev_create_project.step2: Enter the Project Name. Description is optional.
dev_create_project.interface_intro: This interface appears when a user chooses to create a new project from the dashboard. Key elements include:
dev_create_project.f1_desc: A text input where the user enters the name of their new project, e.g., "My Awesome App." This is a required field.
dev_create_project.f2_desc: An optional text area for a brief description of the project, helping to clarify its purpose or scope.
dev_create_project.f3_1: Create Project – Confirms the creation of the project and adds it to the dashboard.
dev_create_project.f3_2: Cancel – Closes the popup without creating a project.
dev_create_project.f4_desc: The popup is centered with a minimal, distraction-free layout that clearly separates it from the blurred background dashboard.
dev_create_project.f5_desc: Placeholder text in the fields guides the user on what information to provide.
dev_create_project.outro: This popup simplifies the process of starting a new project, ensuring users can quickly add and document their projects within the dashboard environment.

dev_project_settings.title: Getting Started
dev_project_settings.intro: This module is the essential configuration phase where you define the core identity and technical DNA of your application. This section bridges the gap between conceptualization and development by establishing the project's parameters, security protocols, and integration points.
dev_project_settings.obj1_desc: By selecting your specific Technology Stack (such as Node.js, Python, or C#), you calibrate the AI agents to generate code and documentation compatible with your preferred ecosystem.
dev_project_settings.obj2_desc: Configure API Keys and Version Control to ensure your project is securely connected to external services and your codebase is tracked from day one.
dev_project_settings.obj3_desc: Utilize the Reverse Engineer tool to ingest existing data or code, allowing the platform to understand and build upon your current technical foundation rather than starting from scratch.
dev_project_settings.submenus_title: Getting started has the following core submenus:
dev_project_settings.sm1_desc: This is the command center for your application's identity. Use this section to define your Project Name and Description, and most importantly, configure your Technology Stack. By selecting your specific backend (e.g., Node.js), database, and frontend frameworks, you provide the necessary context for the AI agents to generate code and documentation that aligns with your environment.
dev_project_settings.sm2_desc: Manage your secure connections and third-party integrations here. This section allows you to generate, store, and rotate Authentication Keys required for external services. Ensuring your API keys are correctly configured is essential for enabling seamless communication between your project and outside platforms.
dev_project_settings.sm3_desc: Connect your project to your preferred Git provider (such as GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket). This section handles the synchronization between your local development environment and your remote repositories. You can track branch deployments, manage commits, and ensure that your codebase is properly versioned as you progress.
dev_project_settings.sm4_desc: Accelerate your development by importing existing assets. This tool allows you to upload current codebases, database schemas, or documentation so the platform can "read" your existing work. It maps out your current architecture, making it easier to build new features that are consistent with your established logic and structure.
dev_project_settings.ps_intro: The Project Settings page is where you establish the high-level identity of your project and calibrate the AI engine. The selections made here act as a "North Star" for all subsequent automated generations, ensuring that the code, diagrams, and business logic produced later are compatible with your specific environment.
dev_project_settings.ps_1_desc: This subsection defines the metadata for your project.
dev_project_settings.ps_1_f1_desc: Enter a unique identifier for your application (e.g., Study_Tool). This name will appear across all exported documentation and reports.
dev_project_settings.ps_1_f2_desc: Provide a concise summary of the project's purpose. A well-defined description helps the AI contextualize the functional requirements and user roles it will generate later in the process.
dev_project_settings.ps_2_desc: The Technology Stack is the most critical component of the settings. It guides the AI agents in producing framework-specific syntax and architectural patterns.
dev_project_settings.ps_2_1_desc: Select the primary language and framework that will power your application logic. The system supports a wide array of environments, including:
dev_project_settings.ps_2_1_l1: JavaScript/TypeScript: Node.js (Express, NestJS, Fastify, Koa, Hapi, AdonisJS).
dev_project_settings.ps_2_1_l2: Python: Django, Flask, FastAPI, Tornado, Pyramid, Bottle, Sanic.
dev_project_settings.ps_2_1_l3: PHP: Laravel, Symfony, Slim, CodeIgniter, Yii, CakePHP, Lumen.
dev_project_settings.ps_2_1_l4: C#: ASP.NET Core, ASP.NET MVC, Web API, Blazor, Minimal API.
dev_project_settings.ps_2_2_desc: Identify the data persistence layer for your project. This selection informs how Data Objects and Database Schemas are visualized and scripted (e.g., SQL-based relational models vs. NoSQL document structures).
dev_project_settings.ps_2_3_desc: The UI/Frontend section is dedicated to defining the "Client Side" of your application. This configuration ensures that the AI-generated user interfaces, component libraries, and frontend logic are consistent with modern web or mobile standards.
dev_project_settings.bp_desc: To ensure the highest accuracy in your project outputs, follow these guidelines:
dev_project_settings.bp_1_desc: Selecting a specific framework (like Node.js - NestJS) rather than a generic runtime (like Node.js) allows the system to generate more precise boilerplate code and folder structures.
dev_project_settings.bp_2_desc: Always click the Save Changes button in the top-right corner before navigating to other sections like API Keys or Business Flows to avoid losing your configuration.
dev_project_settings.bp_3_desc: You can return to this section at any time to update your stack if your project requirements pivot during the ideation phase.

dev_api_keys.title: API Keys
dev_api_keys.intro: The API Keys section is the central hub for managing your application's secure access to integrated AI models and third-party services. This page is split into two primary areas:
dev_api_keys.area1_desc: Where you can view, create, and secure your personal API keys.
dev_api_keys.area2_desc: Where you can track api requests, token consumption, and associated costs across all active models in real-time.
dev_api_keys.access_note: Access this section from the main sidebar under the Getting Started category.
dev_api_keys.managing_intro: This section, found at the top of the page, lists all API keys currently configured for your account.
dev_api_keys.viewing_keys_desc: If no keys have been set up yet, the dashboard will display a key icon with the message "No personal API keys configured."
dev_api_keys.creating_keys_step1: Click the blue "+ Add New Key" button.
dev_api_keys.creating_keys_step2: Follow the prompt to name and configure your new API key.
dev_api_keys.creating_keys_step3: Warning: Ensure you copy and securely store the key upon creation. For security reasons, the full key may not be displayed again.
dev_api_keys.dashboard_intro: The Usage Dashboard provides granular, real-time analytics on your project's interaction with the integrated AI services (e.g., Claude, Gemini).
dev_api_keys.dashboard_3_1_intro: Located in the top-right corner of the dashboard panel.
dev_api_keys.dashboard_3_1_f1_desc: Use the dropdown menu (e.g., "Last 30 days") to change the period for which data is displayed.
dev_api_keys.dashboard_3_1_f2_desc: Click this icon to instantly update all dashboard metrics with the latest data.
dev_api_keys.dashboard_3_2_intro: Four key metric cards summarize the activity for the selected timeframe.
dev_api_keys.dashboard_3_2_f1_desc: The aggregate number of distinct calls made to all API models.
dev_api_keys.dashboard_3_2_f2_desc: The total combined number of tokens processed (Prompt + Completion).
dev_api_keys.dashboard_3_2_f3_desc: The estimated financial cost, in USD, accrued based on the current usage period.
dev_api_keys.dashboard_3_2_f4_desc: The number of tokens retrieved from the cache, which often saves time and cost.
dev_api_keys.analyzing_intro: Below the key metric cards are detailed visualizations and tables for deeper analysis.
dev_api_keys.analyzing_4_1_intro: This bar chart illustrates your Daily Usage (Tokens) over time.
dev_api_keys.analyzing_4_1_f1_desc: Hover over individual bars to see the exact token breakdown for that specific day, segmented by model.
dev_api_keys.analyzing_4_1_f2_desc: The legend (e.g., claude-opus-4-x, gemini-1-5-pro-preview) shows which models are contributing to the daily volume.
dev_api_keys.analyzing_4_2_intro: This detailed table breaks down performance metrics by specific AI model:
model_cols: MODEL: The name and provider of the AI model.
REQUESTS: Number of requests made to this specific model.
PROMPT TOKENS: Tokens sent to the model (input).
COMPLETION TOKENS: Tokens generated by the model (output).
TOTAL TOKENS: The sum of prompt and completion tokens.
CACHE READ/WRITE: Tokens retrieved from cache versus those written to cache.
AVG TIME: Average latency (response time) for requests.
COST: The specific cost associated with using that individual model.
dev_api_keys.transaction_intro: Located at the bottom of the page (scroll down), this table provides a high-level transaction log for every single request.
dev_api_keys.filtering_f1_desc: Filter the log to show only transactions for a specific model (e.g., view only Claude logs).
dev_api_keys.filtering_f2_desc: Filter by transaction status (e.g., view only "Success" or "Error" transactions).
log_cols: TIME: The exact timestamp of the request.
TOTAL TOKENS: The tokens used in that specific request.
CACHE READ/WRITE: Cache performance for that specific request.
TIME (MS): The total execution time of the request in milliseconds.
COST: The exact cost, in USD, incurred by this single transaction.

dev_vc.title: Version Control
dev_vc.intro: The Version Control page is where you manage your project's integration with Git-based platforms (e.g., GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket). By connecting a repository, you enable the platform to push updates, track changes, and maintain a "Source of Truth" for your application's codebase.
dev_vc.table_intro: The main dashboard displays all active repository connections. The table includes the following key information:
dev_vc.table_f1_desc: The name of the connection (e.g., Main Repository). A folder icon indicates the specific directory mapping within the repository.
dev_vc.table_f2_desc: The full URL path to your remote Git repository (e.g., https://gitlab.com/.../studytool.git).
dev_vc.table_f3_desc: The specific branch currently targeted for synchronization (e.g., main).
dev_vc.table_f4_desc: Indicates the current health of the connection. A CONNECTED badge signifies that the platform has successful read/write access.
dev_vc.table_f5_desc: Displays the timestamp of the most recent data exchange between the platform and your repository.
dev_vc.actions_intro: On the right side of each integration row, you will find several quick-action icons to manage your repository:
dev_vc.actions_f1_desc: View a log of previous synchronization events, including commits and updates.
dev_vc.actions_f2_desc: Manually trigger a data transfer. Use Push to send platform changes to your Git provider, or Pull to update the platform with external changes.
dev_vc.actions_f3_desc: Modify the connection settings, such as changing the target branch or updating credentials.
dev_vc.actions_f4_desc: Remove the integration from the project. Note: This will stop synchronization but will not delete the code from your Git provider.
dev_vc.bp_1_desc: It is highly recommended to use a dedicated development or "staging" branch rather than your primary production branch to safely review generated code before merging.
dev_vc.bp_2_desc: Always verify the Last Sync time before starting a new design or ideation session to ensure you are working with the most recent version of your project.
dev_vc.bp_3_desc: Use the Refresh icon in the top-right corner of the panel to update the connection status if you have recently made changes on your Git provider's website.

dev_re.title: Reverse Engineering
dev_re.overview_desc: The Reverse Engineer module utilizes AI-powered analysis to deconstruct your existing codebase, database schemas, or legacy documentation. It transforms raw data into Structured Concepts, allowing the platform to understand your current logic and build upon it without manual data entry.
dev_re.tasks_intro: This dashboard manages your reverse engineering workflows.
dev_re.tasks_f1_desc: If no analysis has been performed, the screen displays "No reverse engineering tasks yet." * Process Flow: Once a task is initiated, this area will track the progress of the AI's analysis, displaying task names, timestamps, and completion statuses.
dev_re.features_f1_desc: Upload or link existing source code to identify data structures, API endpoints, and business logic.
dev_re.features_f2_desc: Ingest database SQL files to automatically generate Data Objects within the platform.
dev_re.features_f3_desc: The AI converts its findings into high-level concepts that populate the Concept, Business Flows, and Data Objects sections in the sidebar.
dev_re.start_step1: Click the blue "Start Reverse Engineer" button (found both in the center of the screen and the top-right corner).
dev_re.start_step2: Select your Source Type (e.g., File Upload, Git Repository Link, or Database Connection).
dev_re.start_step3: Configure the Scope of the analysis by selecting specific folders or tables to include.
dev_re.start_step4: Launch the task and monitor the Diffusion Vision Tasks list for real-time updates.
dev_re.why_f1_desc: Quickly bring an existing project or legacy system into the platform for modernization.
dev_re.why_f2_desc: Ensure that new features developed in the Development section are 100% compatible with your existing data structures.
dev_re.why_f3_desc: Generate fresh Requirements Docs and Technical Diagrams for projects that currently lack them.

dev_struc.title: Structure & Ideation
dev_struc.intro_1: The Structure & Ideation section is the architectural heart of the platform. It is here that the high-level goals defined in your Requirements are decomposed into functional, technical, and logical building blocks.
dev_struc.intro_2: This section serves as a bridge, turning "what" the system should do into "how" it will actually work.

dev_req.submenu_title: Structure & Ideation has the following submenus:
dev_req.title: 1. Requirements
dev_req.intro: As the entry point for this category, this page stores the core vision of your project. It acts as the "Source of Truth." Every other item in this menu is derived from the text provided here.
dev_req.f1_desc: Define your MVP (Minimum Viable Product) features and user roles.
dev_req.action_desc: In this section, you can modify or add new requirements. Once all good, click on Save Requirements button.

dev_concept.title: 2. Concept
dev_concept.intro: This module breaks your requirements down into individual Concept Blocks. For a project like "Study Tool," concepts might include User Authentication, Document Parsing, or Adaptive Algorithm.
dev_concept.f1_desc: Map out the "big ideas" of your software to ensure all required functionality is accounted for.

dev_bf.title: 3. Business Flows
dev_bf.intro: Business Flows visualize the step-by-step journey a user takes to complete a task.
dev_bf.f1_desc: The sequence of events from a student uploading a PDF to the AI generating a summary and quiz.
dev_bf.f2_desc: Identify bottlenecks in the user experience before a single line of code is written.

dev_do.title: 4. Data Objects
dev_do.intro: This section defines the "nouns" of your system—the data structures that will be stored in your database.
dev_do.f2_desc: Define properties (e.g., email, timestamp, score) and how these objects relate to one another.

dev_roles.title: 5. Roles & Permissions
dev_roles.intro: Security starts here. Define what different user types can see and do within the application.
dev_roles.f1_desc: An Admin can delete users, while a Student can only view their own study materials.
dev_roles.f2_desc: Establish the Access Control List (ACL) for your API and Frontend.

dev_br.title: 6. Business Rules
dev_br.intro: Business Rules are the "if/then" logic that governs your app's behavior, independent of the code.
dev_br.f1_desc: "If a user has a Free tier account, they can only upload 1 document per month."
dev_br.f2_desc: Centralize the logic that determines how the business operates.

dev_int.title: 7. Integration Maps
dev_int.intro: This tool outlines how your application communicates with external systems.
dev_int.f1_desc: How your app talks to a Payment Gateway (Stripe) or an AI Model (OpenAI/Claude).
dev_int.f2_desc: Visualize the data exchange points between your system and the outside world.

dev_dd.title: Design and Docs
dev_dd.intro_1: Design & Docs, serves as a centralized hub for managing project-related visual and technical documentation. It provides a quick overview of the assets currently associated with your project.
dev_dd.sub1_desc: This submenu is your primary workspace for all visual assets related to the user interface. It is currently highlighted, indicating it is the active view.
dev_dd.sub1_p: Stores high-fidelity mockups, wireframes, and interactive prototypes.
dev_dd.sub1_b: Reviewing screen layouts, branding elements, and user flow animations.
dev_dd.sub2_desc: This submenu organizes the structural and logic-based visualizations of the project.
dev_dd.sub2_p: Houses system architecture maps, flowcharts, entity-relationship diagrams (ERDs), and sequence diagrams.
dev_dd.sub2_b: Understanding the underlying technical framework and how data moves through the system.
dev_dd.sub3_desc: This submenu acts as the repository for formal project documentation and written specifications.

dev_ui.title: UI Designs
dev_ui.intro: The UI Designs submenu serves as the primary repository for the project's visual interface assets. It is designed to bridge the gap between conceptual design and development by providing a central location for high-fidelity visuals.
dev_ui.cf1_desc: Access all screen designs, from initial wireframes to polished, high-fidelity mockups.
dev_ui.cf2_desc: Locate interactive prototypes to review user journeys and interface animations.
dev_ui.cf3_desc: View the total count of design files (currently 30) at a glance via the numerical indicator on the right.
dev_ui.ug1_desc: When selected, this submenu is highlighted with a soft purple background and blue text, indicating that the files currently displayed in the main content area belong to this category.
dev_ui.ug2_desc: Use the Monitor icon to quickly identify this section in the sidebar.
dev_ui.bp: Best Practice: Keep this section updated with the latest design iterations to ensure the development team is working from the most recent "Source of Truth" for the user interface.
dev_ui.view_look_prompt: Click on each folder to view how it looks:
dev_ui.typo_intro: The Typography Editor is a specialized configuration panel that allows for precise control over the visual appearance of text elements within the interface—specifically targeting the span.sidebar-nav-text element in this view.
dev_ui.t_c1_2: Adjust the font color using an RGB value (currently rgb(191, 219, 254)) or by selecting a shade from the integrated color picker.
dev_ui.t_c2_2: Set the text dimensions using the numerical input or the slider, ranging from 6px to 120px.
dev_ui.t_c3_2: Define the thickness of the characters (currently set to 500 Medium).
dev_ui.t_c4_2: Choose the typeface from a prioritized stack (e.g., Inter, -apple-system).
dev_ui.t_c5_2: Toggle font styles like Italic or decorations such as Underline or None.
dev_ui.t_c6_2: Control the horizontal placement of the text (e.g., start, center, or end).
dev_ui.ke1_desc: The blue header area displays the exact CSS selector path (div.sidebar-nav > ...), ensuring you know exactly which element is being modified.
dev_ui.ke2_desc: Use the Save button to commit your styling changes to the live project, or click the "X" to discard changes and exit the editor.
dev_ui.ke3_desc: Easily switch between pixel (px) and other relative units for font sizing to ensure responsive design compliance.
dev_ui.typo_note: Note: Changes made in this panel are often applied globally to all elements sharing the same class, maintaining visual consistency across the sidebar navigation.
dev_ui.overlay_prompt: An overlay window will be displayed
dev_ui.resp_intro: In the top-center of the interface, you can find the device toggle icons (Desktop, Tablet, and Mobile) used to preview how the layout adapts to various screen sizes.
dev_ui.resp_desk_desc: The default current view – this is optimized for large screens, offering a spacious and organized layout.
dev_ui.resp_tab_desc: In this mode, the interface shifts to accommodate mid-sized touchscreens.
dev_ui.resp_mob_desc: The mobile view prioritizes vertical stacking to ensure readability on small screens.
dev_ui.mpa_intro: The Modify Page overlay is an AI-powered conversational interface designed to help you make rapid design and structural changes to your project. By selecting specific elements and describing your desired outcome, the assistant automates the CSS or HTML adjustments for you.
dev_ui.t2_c1_2: The message bubble at the top provides status updates and prompts you for specific design instructions.
dev_ui.t2_c2_2: The blue tag (e.g., span.sidebar-nav-text) indicates exactly which part of the page is currently being targeted for modification.
dev_ui.t2_c3_2: The primary text area where you can type natural language commands like "Make this text bold" or "Change the font color to dark red."
dev_ui.t2_c4_2: Send initiates the request, while Undo allows you to revert the last change immediately.
dev_ui.kf1_desc: The assistant knows which element you have selected, allowing you to give short commands without re-specifying the target.
dev_ui.kf2_desc: As you type, the interface provides a placeholder hint reminding you of the active selection.
dev_ui.kf3_desc: The presence of the Undo button ensures you can experiment with different styles safely.
dev_ui.htu1_desc: Click an element on the underlying page to focus the assistant's attention.
dev_ui.htu2_desc: Type your desired change into the input box (e.g., "Increase the padding and add a border").
dev_ui.htu3_desc: Click Send to see the changes applied in real-time.

tech_diag.title: Technical Diagrams
tech_diag.intro: The Technical Diagrams dashboard is a comprehensive visual management suite designed for architects, developers, and system planners. It serves as the central repository for all logic-based and structural visualizations of a project, ensuring that technical requirements and workflows are clearly documented.
tech_diag.grid_intro: The interface displays a grid of Diagram Cards, each representing a specific technical perspective of the system. Each card includes:
tech_diag.b2_desc: An "ACTIVE" badge confirms the diagram is current and validated.
tech_diag.b3_desc: Uses industry-standard visual markers for quick identification.

avail_diag.title: Available Diagram Types
avail_diag.intro: The dashboard is divided into several specialized categories to cover the full development lifecycle:
avail_diag.t_c1_2: Technology Stack, Security Architecture, Infrastructure, Deployment, High-Level System Architecture.
avail_diag.t_c2_2: Entity Relationship Diagram (ERD), Database Schema, Data Flow Diagram (DFD).
avail_diag.t_c3_2: Sequence Diagram, Process Flow / Business Flow, Use Case Diagram, User Flow Diagram.
avail_diag.kac_intro: Located at the top-right of the dashboard, these controls allow for active project management:
avail_diag.k1_desc: Launches the automated builder tool to generate diagrams based on existing system data or code structures.
avail_diag.k2_desc: Opens a manual upload or creation interface to introduce a new visualization to the project.
avail_diag.ad_intro: This button is for manual entry and custom creation.
avail_diag.ad_hw_desc: It opens a template gallery or a blank canvas where you can choose a specific diagram type (e.g., Sequence Diagram, ERD, or Flowchart).
avail_diag.ad_res_desc: You manually define the logic, shapes, and connections. It can also be used to upload an existing image file (like a PNG or PDF) from your local drive.
avail_diag.ad_best_desc: Conceptualizing new ideas, documenting specific business rules, or adding external reference diagrams that aren't derived from the project's code.
avail_diag.bad_intro: This button triggers an automated generation process.
avail_diag.bad_hw_desc: It uses the platform's AI or system analysis tools to scan your existing project data, codebases, or infrastructure configurations.
avail_diag.bad_res_desc: It automatically creates a "High-Level System Architecture" or "Technology Stack" diagram based on actual project facts.
avail_diag.bad_best_desc: Rapidly documenting complex systems without drawing every line manually, or keeping diagrams in sync with real-time code changes.
avail_diag.idv_intro: The Interactive Viewer allows you to transition from a high-level overview of your project's architecture to a detailed, editable workspace for any specific technical diagram.
avail_diag.nw_s1_desc: From the Technical Diagrams dashboard, click on any of the active diagram cards (e.g., Data Flow Diagram [DFD]).
avail_diag.nw_s2_desc: The system launches a dedicated canvas overlay, centered on that specific data model or workflow.
avail_diag.nw_s3_desc: Use the sidebar on the left to quickly jump between different diagram types within the same session without returning to the main dashboard.
avail_diag.wc_r1_2: Located at the top, this bar provides drag-and-drop components specific to the diagram type, such as Start, Process, Decision, Database, and API nodes.
avail_diag.wc_r2_2: The primary area where the workflow is visualized. It supports complex branching, layered layouts, and multi-node connections.
avail_diag.wc_r3_2: Lists all available diagram categories for the current project. Numerical indicators show how many iterations or sub-diagrams exist for each type (e.g., High-Level System Architecture: 42).
avail_diag.wc_r4_2: A specialized set of buttons for canvas management, including Auto Layout, Voice Assistant, Clear All, and zoom controls (100%, Fit).
avail_diag.kf_ce_desc: The toolbar automatically updates based on the diagram type selected, ensuring you use the correct notation (e.g., diamonds for Decisions in a DFD).
avail_diag.kf_lm_desc: Use the Layered Layout and Direction (Top to Bottom) dropdowns to instantly reorganize complex "spaghetti" diagrams into clean, readable structures.
avail_diag.kf_ce2_desc: Once your diagram is finalized, use the Export button to download the visualization for external documentation or presentations.
avail_diag.idv_tip: Tip: Enable the AI Assistant to make rapid changes to your diagrams using natural language commands, similar to the Modify Page tool.
avail_diag.tb_intro: The top toolbar in the Diagram Editor is divided into three functional zones: Node Creation, Layout Management, and Canvas Utilities. These tools allow you to build and organize complex data flows with precision.
avail_diag.nct_intro: These buttons add specific logic elements to your canvas. Each follows standard technical diagramming notation:
avail_diag.nct1_desc: Inserts a terminal node representing the beginning of a process or entry point.
avail_diag.nct2_desc: Adds a rectangular node to represent a specific action, computation, or system task.
avail_diag.nct3_desc: Inserts a diamond-shaped node used for branching logic and "If/Then" scenarios.
avail_diag.nct4_desc: Adds a cylinder-shaped node representing data storage, tables, or repositories.
avail_diag.nct5_desc: Inserts a node specifically for external service integrations or interface endpoints.
avail_diag.nct6_desc: Represents the exit point or completion of a specific workflow.
avail_diag.ldc_intro: Use these dropdowns to define the global visual structure of your diagram:
avail_diag.ldc1_desc: Choose between different algorithmic styles, such as "Layered," "Tree," or "Organic," to best fit your data's structure.
avail_diag.ldc2_desc: Sets the flow orientation (e.g., Top to Bottom, Left to Right) to ensure logical readability.
avail_diag.cus_intro: These buttons provide advanced management and accessibility features:
avail_diag.cus1_desc: Instantly snaps all nodes and connectors into an organized grid based on your selected direction and layout style.
avail_diag.cus2_desc: Toggles a background grid to assist with manual alignment and spacing.
avail_diag.cus3_desc: Activates AI-driven support, allowing you to modify the diagram or add elements using natural language commands.
avail_diag.cus4_desc: Wipes the entire canvas to start a new design from scratch.
avail_diag.ne1_desc: Adjust your view of the canvas. Fit automatically scales the diagram to fill the visible screen area.
avail_diag.ne2_desc: Saves the current visualization as a high-resolution image (PNG/SVG) or a document (PDF) for external use.

req_docs.title: Requirements Docs
req_docs.intro: The Requirements Documents dashboard is the central repository for your project's formal specifications and documentation. This page allows you to manage, review, and regenerate key documents that define the project's scope, functionality, and technical framework.
req_docs.adt_intro: The dashboard organizes documentation into four primary categories, each serving a specific role in the project lifecycle:
req_docs.t_r1_3: Visual overviews and core value propositions.
req_docs.t_r2_3: Detailed requirements, user stories, and feature logic.
req_docs.t_r3_3: System architecture, API documentation, and implementation details.
req_docs.t_r4_3: Database schemas, entity relationships (ERD), and tables.
req_docs.dsm_intro: Each document card features real-time status indicators and management tools:
req_docs.dsm_b1_desc: A green "Ready" indicator confirms that the document has been successfully generated and is up to date with the latest project changes.
req_docs.dsm_b2_desc: Click this button to view the full document in the built-in reader or to download a local copy.
req_docs.dsm_b3_desc: This action triggers the AI engine to scan your current project data—including diagrams, tasks, and code—to regenerate the document, ensuring it remains synchronized with your latest work.
req_docs.dsm_b4_desc: Use the refresh button at the top-right of the page to check for global status updates across all document types.
req_docs.kw_1_desc: When you first start a project, use Rebuild to create your initial specifications from your concept sketches or technical diagrams.
req_docs.kw_2_desc: Check the Ready status before sharing documents with stakeholders to ensure they are finalized.
req_docs.kw_3_desc: If you modify your Technical Diagrams or UI Designs, return here and click Rebuild on the corresponding document to keep your documentation in sync with your designs.
req_docs.dev_intro: The Development section is the technical engine of your project, providing direct access to the backend infrastructure, project management tasks, and the actual codebase. This section is designed for developers and engineers to manage the build and deployment phases of the project lifecycle.
req_docs.dev_sub1_desc: The project's central action log, containing all developmental "To-Dos," bug reports, and feature requests.
req_docs.dev_sub2_desc: Access to the data layer, including table structures, stored procedures, and data migration scripts.
req_docs.dev_sub3_desc: A repository for the application's logic, scripts, and front-end/back-end programming files.
req_docs.dev_sub4_desc: Infrastructure management for deployment environments (e.g., Docker or Kubernetes configurations).

tasks.title: Tasks
tasks.intro: Tasks dashboard is the high-velocity command center where your project requirements are translated into actionable development steps. It provides a real-time audit trail of every operation performed by the AI agents—from deep-system analysis to front-end code implementation.
tasks.knc_b1_desc: Easily toggle between Pending, In Progress, Completed, and Failed tasks to monitor project health.
tasks.knc_b2_1_desc: Sync the task queue with current agent activity.
tasks.knc_b2_2_desc: Manage your task history and halt active processes.
tasks.knc_b2_3_desc: Manually trigger a new objective, such as a code fix or a new feature request.
tasks.aoc_intro: Every task provides deep transparency into the AI's "thought process" and execution:
tasks.aoc_r1_2: Displays the specific instruction being executed (e.g., "Perform a comprehensive gap analysis").
tasks.aoc_r2_2: Identifies which specialized agent is working on the task (e.g., Analytical Agent, Task Manager Agent, or Developer Agent).
tasks.aoc_r3_2: Shows the specific project folder (e.g., studytool) and the date of execution.
tasks.aoc_r4_2: Blue information boxes break down complex, multi-phase plans (e.g., "This plan was created in 10 phases") to show exactly how the AI is approaching the problem.
tasks.aoc_r5_2: Special badges like Analysis (purple) or Code (blue) indicate the nature of the task's result.
tasks.pmf_b1_desc: The system can automatically compare your Existing Schema against your Concept Design to identify missing tables or logic gaps.
tasks.pmf_b2_desc: Use the + New Subtask button to decompose large architectural goals into granular, manageable technical steps.
tasks.pmf_b3_desc: Icons for Edit, Rerun, and Delete allow you to iterate on specific prompts until the output matches your exact vision.
tasks.pmf_b4_desc: Tasks provide a transparent look at the specific files being read or modified, including file paths and sizes (e.g., authController.js - 17290 bytes).
tasks.tc_intro: Task Controls found in the upper right corner of the Project Tasks dashboard. They allow you to manage the entire AI agent queue with a single click.
tasks.tc_refresh_4: If you've just submitted a task or are waiting for an agent to finish a complex coding operation, hit Refresh to see the most current status (e.g., moving from "In Progress" to "Completed").
tasks.tc_cancel_2: Immediately halts all active and pending agent operations.
tasks.tc_cancel_4: Use this if you realize a prompt was incorrect or if the AI is heading in a technical direction you want to pivot away from. It prevents the system from wasting resources on tasks you no longer need.
tasks.tc_delete_2: Clears your entire task history from the dashboard.
tasks.tc_delete_4: Best for "cleaning house" once a major milestone—like the TransparencySeal core architecture—is finished and verified. Note: This action is permanent and clears the audit trail.
tasks.tc_new_4: This is your primary entry point for delegating work. Use it to trigger everything from a simple UI change to a massive database audit.
tasks.cnt_intro: The New Task modal is where you delegate specific objectives to specialized AI agents. To ensure the best results, you must provide a clear description, select the appropriate agent for the job, and associate the task with the correct project folder.
tasks.cnt_1_intro: This text area is for your natural language instructions.
tasks.cnt_1_b1_desc: Instead of saying "Fix the login," try "Fix the authentication logic in authController.js to prevent duplicate session tokens."
tasks.cnt_1_b2_desc: Mention specific database tables, UI components, or documentation files you want the agent to consider.
tasks.cnt_2_intro: Selecting the right "specialist" ensures the AI uses the correct toolset for the job.
tasks.cnt_t2_r1_2: Executes command-line instructions, installs dependencies, and manages file systems.
tasks.cnt_t2_r2_2: Writes, refactors, and debugs application code (HTML, CSS, JS, etc.).
tasks.cnt_t2_r3_2: Manages schemas, writes SQL queries, and handles data migrations.
tasks.cnt_t2_r4_2: Focuses on UI/UX layouts, slide decks, and visual concept assets.
tasks.cnt_t2_r5_2: Drafts and updates Functional Specs, Technical Specs, and User Manuals.
tasks.cnt_t2_r6_2: Orchestrates Docker/Kubernetes settings and deployment environments.
tasks.cnt_t2_r7_2: High-level planning; breaks down complex goals into a multi-phase roadmap.
tasks.cnt_t2_r8_2: Performs quality assurance, identifies UI inconsistencies, and checks logic flows.
tasks.cnt_t2_r9_2: Generates video tutorials or walkthroughs based on project state.
tasks.cnt_t2_r10_2: Performs "Read-Only" deep dives, such as Gap Analysis or security audits.
tasks.cnt_t2_r11_2: Scans for vulnerabilities and ensures encryption/authentication compliance.
tasks.cnt_t2_r12_2: Automatically generates unit tests and end-to-end testing scripts.
tasks.cnt_3_intro: Choose the project directory where the agent should focus its work.
tasks.cnt_3_b1: In your current view, the "selected project" folder is selected.
tasks.cnt_3_b2: This ensures the agent doesn't pull context from unrelated projects, keeping the current project's logic separate from other builds.
tasks.cnt_fa_1_desc: Discards the draft and returns you to the Project Tasks dashboard.
tasks.cnt_fa_2_desc: Submits the prompt to the AI queue. You can track its progress immediately on the main dashboard under the Pending or In Progress tabs.

database.title: Database
database.intro: The Database Connections module is the technical foundation of your application. In a high-integrity project like TransparencySeal, this is where the "Source of Truth" is defined and managed. It transforms a raw database into a visual, browsable asset that the AI can use to write code and generate reports.
database.act_b1_desc: It packages your entire database (tables, constraints, and rows) into a single .sql file and downloads it to your computer.
database.act_b2_desc: Saves the database schema directly into the project's internal file system.
database.act_b3_desc: Activates the Schema Explorer for the selected connection.
database.td_intro: When you click a database block (or "tile") from the main inventory, the Table Details overlay acts as a deep-dive X-ray into that specific entity. This view is critical for auditing the structural integrity of your application, ensuring that the AI has mapped your business logic correctly.
database.ts_r1_2: The unique name for each data field (e.g., claim_number, loss_type).
database.ts_r2_2: The technical format of the data. Notice int for numbers, varchar for text, date for time, and decimal(15,2) for precise currency values.
database.ts_r3_2: Indicates if the field can be left empty (Yes) or if it is mandatory (No). Fields like claim_id and policy_id are mandatory to ensure data integrity.
database.ts_r4_2: The value assigned if no data is provided (e.g., status defaults to 'OPEN').
database.ts_r5_2: Represents the Primary Key (the gold key icon 🔑). This is the unique identifier (like a social security number) for that specific record.

source_code.title: Source Code
source_code.intro: The Source Code dashboard is where your project transitions from high-level "Concept" to actual digital infrastructure. This is a live file system where Think4ever manages every script, style, and configuration file of any project.
source_code.ns_intro: The Files Browser provides a standard, intuitive directory structure. In your current view (the insurance folder), the AI has organized the project into logical sections:
source_code.ns_b1_desc: Contains environment settings and database connection strings.
source_code.ns_b2_desc: The "Front Door" of your app—houses the HTML, CSS, and images the user actually sees.
source_code.ns_b3_desc: The "Traffic Controller"—defines the paths (URLs) for your application (e.g., /dashboard or /expenditures).
source_code.ns_b4_desc: Helper functions and small scripts that handle repetitive tasks.
source_code.apf_intro: Every file listed includes its Type, Size, and the Last Modified date, giving you a clear audit trail of development.
source_code.apf_r1_2: A snapshot of your database structure, often created by the "Export to Source" function.
source_code.apf_r2_2: The main entry point for your backend server—this is the "brain" that connects your UI to your data.
source_code.apf_r3_2: A manifest file that lists all the technical dependencies your project needs to run.
source_code.apf_r4_2: The project's documentation, detailing how to install and use the application.
source_code.apf_outro: In the Source Code section, navigating and managing your technical assets is designed to be as intuitive as a standard desktop environment, but with high-end developer tools built right in.
source_code.nt_b1_desc: Use the dynamic path links (e.g., Root / public / assets) at the top of the browser to move instantly between parent and child directories.
source_code.nt_b2_desc: Every asset is listed with its Name, Type (JS, HTML, CSS, etc.), Size, and the exact Timestamp of the last modification. This provides a clear audit trail for every change the AI agents make.
source_code.ifa_intro: For surgical control over your application's files, each row in the browser offers four primary actions:
source_code.ifa_b1_d1_desc: Opens a quick-look preview of the file's content.
source_code.ifa_b1_d2_desc: Perfect for a "spot check" to verify that a logic fix or a UI update was applied correctly without accidentally changing anything.
source_code.ifa_b2_d1_desc: Launches a professional-grade development environment featuring the high-contrast Catppuccin theme.
source_code.ifa_b2_d2_desc: Features full syntax highlighting, making it easy to manually tweak code or add custom comments. It's the ultimate tool for "Final Form" personalization.
source_code.ifa_b3_d1_desc: Instantly saves the specific file to your computer.
source_code.ifa_b3_d2_desc: Use this if you need to share a specific script with a collaborator or want to keep a local backup of a critical configuration file like db.sql.
source_code.ifa_b4_d1_desc: Removes the file from the project directory.
source_code.ifa_b4_d2_desc: Includes a confirmation step to prevent accidental loss of data. Use this to prune legacy files or experimental scripts that are no longer needed.

containers.title: Containers
containers.intro: The Containers module is where your application transitions from a collection of files into a portable, production-ready system. By leveraging Docker, Think4ever AI ensures that your project — runs identically on your local machine, a staging server, or the cloud.
containers.r1_c2: View your docker-compose.yml file. Toggle to Visual Mode to see your servers as interactive cards.
containers.r2_c2: Don't like a setting? Just tell the AI: "Change the database port to 3306" or "Add a backup service."
containers.r3_c2: A "Time Machine" for your infrastructure. If a change breaks the build, roll back to a previous version instantly.
containers.r4_c2: The final step. Download as ZIP for your local machine or Push to Source to add it to your project files.
